title: Wire New Project button: create modal in /projects UI
implementation_notes: In app/pages/projects/index.vue add: showCreateModal ref (default false), createName ref, createError ref, isCreating ref.
Wire @click on the 'New Project' header button and the empty-state 'Create Project' button to set showCreateModal = true.

Add a modal overlay (fixed inset-0 backdrop-blur-sm) containing a card with: title 'New Project', a single 'Project name' text input bound to createName, an error message span (shown when createError is set), Cancel + Create buttons.
Create button calls POST /api/vault/projects with { name: createName }. On success: close modal, reset refs, call refreshNuxtData(relayhqReadModelKey) to refresh the project list without page reload.
On API error, display the error message inside the modal (do not close it).
The 'New Project' button in the empty state row also opens the same modal.
Do not add the modal to [id].vue — only index.vue.
acceptance_criteria: Typing a name and clicking Create calls POST /api/vault/projects and the new project appears in the table immediately.
Clicking Cancel or submitting with empty name shows a validation error without closing.
The rest of the /projects page (search, table, navigation to [id]) is unaffected.
completion_summary: Wired the New Project button and empty-state CTA to an inline modal that creates projects and refreshes the list without a full reload.
